trajectory_summary: A warm referral call that books a Saturday appointment with the spouse included — a structurally sound outcome on a cooperative lead. The rep moved efficiently through verify and reached a real booking. The call's weakness is not in what went wrong but in what was left on the table: the referral energy was never anchored, the average bill was never captured, and the community solar complexity was acknowledged twice without resolution. The appointment is conditional on a bill that hasn't arrived, and the button-up is missing the verbal handshake.
outcome_dispute: The call ends with a booked Saturday appointment at 2PM with the spouse included — that is a genuine positive outcome on a warm referral. However, the booking is explicitly conditional: the rep states confirmation will be sent 'once I get that bill from you' (07:10), meaning the appointment is not locked. The outcome should be tagged as tentative/conditional, not a clean booked sit.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Conditional confirmation — decouple the bill from the booking anchor
primary_coaching_focus.quote: yeah, once I get that bill from you, I'll just send you a, confirmation text or email with the date and time of the meeting.
primary_coaching_focus.why: The appointment is booked but not locked. Nick is at work, said the bill would 'take a little bit to dig that up,' and has no confirmation text in his inbox. If the bill doesn't arrive, there is no confirmation, and the Saturday 2PM slot drifts. The fix is simple and immediate: send the confirmation text now with the Saturday 2PM slot, separately follow up for the bill, and add the 'is that fair?' handshake before hanging up. This is the highest-leverage coaching point because it directly protects the sit — and on a warm referral with a cooperative customer, losing this appointment to a no-show is an avoidable outcome.

key_moments.quote: Yeah. Sounds good. I'd love to help you out with that.
key_moments.stewart_read: Nick has just disclosed a warm referral from an active Ion customer — the highest-trust opening a setter can receive. The rep's response is a generic acknowledgment that immediately pivots to data collection ('So, yeah, we do have referral bonuses... I just need some basic information from you'). The referral energy — why Nick is calling, what his father-in-law told him, what Nick is hoping to learn — is never explored. Strategic read: the rep may have been moving efficiently to qualify before investing time. Reactive read: the rep missed the single best rapport anchor on the call. The reactive read holds — there was no later move that picked this up.

key_moments.quote: I am tied into one of those, like, solar farms where they give you, like, a percentage of the, you know, they're kind of associated with Excel sort of. I I mean but, like, you get part of your energy is being pulled from a solar farm. Is that I'm sure you've heard of those setups.
key_moments.stewart_read: Nick is flagging a genuine complexity — a community solar subscription — and explicitly asking whether the rep is familiar with it ('Is that I'm sure you've heard of those setups'). The rep's response ('Okay. Yes. Sounds good. No problem. I'll go ahead and put that down here.') is a social acknowledgment, not an informational one. The rep doesn't probe what this means for Nick's bill, his current offset percentage, or whether it affects the design. This is a knowledge gap: the rep either doesn't know how community solar interacts with rooftop solar, or doesn't know to flag it for the closer. Either way, the closer walks in blind.

key_moments.quote: You can typically get those in the mail or online?
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep collects the bill format (online) and moves to delivery method. The average monthly bill amount — described in the codex as 'THE anchor for the entire pitch' — was never asked. The rep has Nick's utility company (Xcel), his roof age, his credit, and his address, but has no bill number to anchor the pitch emotionally. The bill collection beat here is purely logistical: get the document, not anchor the pain.

key_moments.quote: And then, from there, we just need a a good time. We can have a local specialist drop by and kinda go over everything with you.
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep moves to scheduling before the bill has been received, then at 07:10 makes the confirmation explicitly conditional: 'once I get that bill from you, I'll just send you a confirmation text or email.' This means the appointment has no confirmation anchor until the bill arrives. If Nick doesn't send the bill — he's at work, said it would 'take a little bit to dig that up' — there is no confirmation text, and the appointment drifts. Additionally, the button-up is missing the 'is that fair' verbal handshake (codex: button_up.is_that_fair_handshake) and the no-driving/at-computer anchor.
